title: Yin Tiger
summary: The Yin Tiger is a character merchant and optional boss battle in Black Myth Wukong. One of the Zodiac Deities that resdies in the Zodiac Village, you'll be able to challenge this fearsome blacksmith to a sparring match, and earn the right transform into him for short periods.

infobox.Location:
In order to reach the Yin Tiger, you must first unlock the Zodiac Village. This can be done in Chapter 3, after arriving at the North Shore of the Bitter Lake. By heading left along the shore, you'll meet Chen Loong, and will also need to defeat him in combat so that he'll calm down enough to ask you for aid.
Get the medicine Chen Loong seeks from the Xu Dog back in Chapter 2 under the Tiger Temple, and you'll be given the Ruyi Scroll to teleport you to the Zodiac Village, a hub of merchants -- many of whom you've already met.
One of these merchants is the Yin Tiger, who serves as a blacksmith to help upgrade your armor, and you can also choose to challenge him to a duel.
Yin Tiger Boss Fight and Guide
Be warned: challenging the Yin Tiger to a duel won't be an easy fight. In fact, even though you can challenge him early on in Chapter 3, it's highly recommend that you hold off on challenging him until you've reach the end of or completed New Thunderclap Temple, and gained additional upgrades, gear, and Spirits to challenge him with. The Yin Tiger is an absolutely ferocious enemy who holds nothing back, and can teleport at will to confuse and disorient you before unleashing incredibly destructive attacks.
There is some good news to this fight: Since it's a "friendly" duel, you won't actually be killed, and instead will simply stop fighting once you lose all of your health -- which will be instantly replenished. The Yin Tiger also gives you a lengthy period at the start to use whatever consumables you have on hand to prepare before the fight truly begins.
Once it does, you'll need to be on top of your game and quickly adapt to his different moves, teleports, and fake-out attacks. His most common attacks come from large two-handed sweeps from his blade, like 3-hit combos ending with an overhead sweep that have huge trails coming from his blade.
Other times, he'll perform a downward slash, followed by an evenly-spaced upward slash, and finish with a spinning 2-hit slash in quick succession.
Some of his basic attacks can be deflected with Rock Solid, but as the fight progresses and the edge of his blade glows red, you'll find less chances to reliably stagger him -- but the deflections can still help you avoid take damage.
Once he gets into his flow, be wary when his blade ignites as he spins into a downward slash, follows up with a quick shoulder bash, and then holds up his sword to block any retaliation strikes.
When he swings his sword down after this, don't dodge early. He'll feint into a leaping overhand spin strike to slam down into the ground, and then close with one last spinning slash to try and prevent you from countering, as the slash has its own wave of force to knock you back.
Soon after, he'll start to begin his teleporting attacks, disappearing into a swirl of leaves and making things a lot harder to predict. He'll now appear from pretty much anywhere, and immediately launch into one of several types of attacks, usually in this order (early on):
A slow overhead slash attack, followed by another slow spinning slash after a short delay. A quick roll into a backhanded spin attack with a fast attack speed. A charged up overhead slam with his burning blade, which he can attack with twice, causing a large explosive detonation a moment after the second strike lands.
Most times, when the Yin Tiger performs his disappearing act, he'll do so for several quick attacks that usually always end with a strong attack like the one mentioned above. Because of this, you should usually try to hold of on retaliating until dodging his explosive finale attacks.
Keep a close eye on his movements, especially when performing his backhanded spin attacks. He'll often feint by spinning once without attacking to bait out your dodge before actually swinging his blade on the second spin.
He'll do the same after one of these feints, charging up his blade as he performs a forward spin, but not unleashing the full force of his charged up attack until the second spin to deal massive damage in a wide area.
Be warned though, for all his disappearing acts, the Yin Tiger is not a fan of your own tricks. Immobilize generally does not work on him, as he'll break free after a second and disappear and curse your dirty tricks, reappearing as a dashes up to launch two slow sweeping strikes. Because of this, you're likely better off using Circle of Fire to grant yourself some breathing room and a quick heal.
Expect him to adapt and chain together more sweeping attacks as the fight goes on, which can make things harder to anticipate when his combos seem to go on forever. They'll usually end with a big bang, like whirling attack he can use that gathers strength with two regular damaging spins first, before unleashing a large final charged uppercut that tears the ground apart in a large line.
Certain times, the swirl of leaves that appear when he vanishes can give you a clue on his next attack. If you see a cluster of leaves appear where he vanishes and fly up above you, it means he's going to perform a dropping slam attack from above, which you can dodge -- but be ready for him to teleport again before using another attack, so don't bother trying to retaliate.
If you spot him holding his blade flat out in front of him, do not fall for his bait. He'll soak the blows of your attacks, and each hit will cause his sword to glow with more power before he takes all of the energy from your attacks to unleash one of his own for massive damage.
Possibly his most annoying attack that can happen is when he runs away before disappearing into the leaves. When he reappears after this, he'll try to appear close by and let out a giant roar. If you are close by and don't dodge away, you'll be stunned, and he'll perform an unblockable grab dealing massive damage.
The only real way to predict this move is to watch at how he vanishes. If he leaps backward, he'll only perform his regular attacks or his drop attack, but if he turns and runs first, it means he's preparing to ambush you with his roar.
The one last surprise he has in store when he gets low on health is his ability to perform a very destructive slash with little warning. He'll employ it after completing many of his other spinning attacks that leaves his sword behind his back.
He'll grab it with two hands and let out a shout, and then dash extremely quickly to close the distance to you, with only a smallest of delays before bringing the flaming sword around to slash upwards in a massive damaging hit.
It'll take a fair bit of practice to understand how his movement works, and how to evade his various sweeps and feint attacks. Make sure to time your Spirit Attacks and Transformation Spells well, as you don't want to waste them during the middle of his teleportation spree. Having a mobile and evasive transformation spell is best, as you'll need to do a fair bit of dodging between his attacks or you'll use up your might.
Once you've finally mastered the duel, the Yin Tiger will angrily concede, tossing his sword to you and vowing to forge another. This sword will grant you the Ebon Flow Transformation Spell , allowing you to become the Yin Tiger! His moves are unique, and allows for a dedicated block to help build your focus and unleash big attacks.
Yin Tiger
In addition to you new spell, you should speak to the Yin Tiger once more. You can now ask him about Curios to get a third slot for your passive talismans, and also unlock a new Iron-Tough Armor Set to craft!
You can also challenge him to more duels, but you won't gain anything for subsequent victories.
infobox.Rewards: Ebon Flow Transformation Spell, Extra Curio Slot

Keep a close eye on his movements, especially when performing his backhanded spin attacks. He'll often feint by spinning once without attacking to bait out your dodge before actually swinging his blade on the second spin. He'll do the same after one of these feints, charging up his blade as he performs a forward spin, but not unleashing the full force of his charged up attack until the second spin to deal massive damage in a wide area. Be warned though, for all his disappearing acts, the Yin Tiger is not a fan of your own tricks. Immobilize generally does not work on him, as he'll break free after a second and disappear and curse your dirty tricks, reappearing as a dashes up to launch two slow sweeping strikes. Because of this, you're likely better off using Circle of Fire to grant yourself some breathing room and a quick heal. Expect him to adapt and chain together more sweeping attacks as the fight goes on, which can make things harder to anticipate when his combos seem to go on forever. They'll usually end with a big bang, like whirling attack he can use that gathers strength with two regular damaging spins first, before unleashing a large final charged uppercut that tears the ground apart in a large line. Certain times, the swirl of leaves that appear when he vanishes can give you a clue on his next attack. If you see a cluster of leaves appear where he vanishes and fly up above you, it means he's going to perform a dropping slam attack from above, which you can dodge -- but be ready for him to teleport again before using another attack, so don't bother trying to retaliate. If you spot him holding his blade flat out in front of him, do not fall for his bait. He'll soak the blows of your attacks, and each hit will cause his sword to glow with more power before he takes all of the energy from your attacks to unleash one of his own for massive damage. Possibly his most annoying attack that can happen is when he runs away before disappearing into the leaves. When he reappears after this, he'll try to appear close by and let out a giant roar. If you are close by and don't dodge away, you'll be stunned, and he'll perform an unblockable grab dealing massive damage. The only real way to predict this move is to watch at how he vanishes. If he leaps backward, he'll only perform his regular attacks or his drop attack, but if he turns and runs first, it means he's preparing to ambush you with his roar. The one last surprise he has in store when he gets low on health is his ability to perform a very destructive slash with little warning. He'll employ it after completing many of his other spinning attacks that leaves his sword behind his back. He'll grab it with two hands and let out a shout, and then dash extremely quickly to close the distance to you, with only a smallest of delays before bringing the flaming sword around to slash upwards in a massive damaging hit. It'll take a fair bit of practice to understand how his movement works, and how to evade his various sweeps and feint attacks. Make sure to time your Spirit Attacks and Transformation Spells well, as you don't want to waste them during the middle of his teleportation spree. Having a mobile and evasive transformation spell is best, as you'll need to do a fair bit of dodging between his attacks or you'll use up your might. Once you've finally mastered the duel, the Yin Tiger will angrily concede, tossing his sword to you and vowing to forge another. This sword will grant you the Ebon Flow Transformation Spell , allowing you to become the Yin Tiger! His moves are unique, and allows for a dedicated block to help build your focus and unleash big attacks. Yin Tiger In addition to you new spell, you should speak to the Yin Tiger once more. You can now ask him about Curios to get a third slot for your passive talismans, and also unlock a new Iron-Tough Armor Set to craft! You can also challenge him to more duels, but you won't gain anything for subsequent victories.
